articles.title: Weather Today [22 March, 2026]: Check IMD Forecast, Snow, Heat Across States & Rain Predictions for Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Jammu & Ahmedabad - The Sunday Guardian
articles.full_text:
Weather Today (22 March 2026): Check IMD forecast for Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Karnataka, Rajasthan, J&K, Ahmedabad, Punjab, Hyderabad & UP. Get city-wise temperature, rain, snow predictions & sunrise-sunset timings.
Weather Today (22 March 2026): India’s weather this March is refusing to settle into a predictable pattern. From sudden drops in temperature to bursts of cloud cover, conditions are shifting rapidly across regions. According to India Meteorological Department, these fluctuations reflect a transitional phase where winter retreats and summer begins to take hold, often producing sharp and uneven changes.
Higher reaches of the Himalayas, especially in Jammu and Kashmir and Himachal regions, may see light snowfall as lower hill areas are likely to remain mostly dry and though patchy rain cannot be ruled out.
Minimum: 17°C | Maximum: 30°C
Partly cloudy skies with mild winds and air quality has improved after recent rainfall.
Minimum: 26°C | Maximum: 33°C
Hot and humid conditions dominate with dry weather expected.
Minimum: 26°C | Maximum: 30°C
Coastal humidity persists and though temperatures remain moderate.
Minimum: 23°C | Maximum: 35°C

Minimum: 15°C | Maximum: 28°C

Minimum: 23°C | Maximum: 34°C
Hot and dry conditions with rising temperatures.
Daytime temperatures are expected to rise by 2–5°C across several regions and this could increase heat stress, impact crops and raise electricity demand as cooling needs grow.
Air quality in Delhi has improved to the moderate range following recent rain and offering temporary relief from pollution levels.
1. Are there any travel or safety warnings?
Fog in northern plains and snowfall in hills may affect travel conditions.
2. Will temperatures rise across the country?
Yes, most regions are expected to see a gradual increase in daytime temperatures.
3. Will there be any rain today?
Rainfall will remain limited, with scattered light showers in select regions.
4. Where will it snow today?
Higher Himalayan regions may receive light snowfall.
5. Is the weather unusual this time?
Yes, rapid shifts indicate increased climate variability during seasonal transition.
